topic: Sharp +-1 implementability of the interference-homophily experimental design SDP (anchor: Optimal Design under Interference, Homophily, and Robustness Trade-offs, arXiv:2601.17145).

open_problems.open_problem: Characterize, for balanced two-community homophily graphs, exactly when the interference-design SDP optimum is attainable as the second-moment matrix of a randomized +-1 assignment.

source_refs.claim: The anchor formulates HT-GATE worst-case-MSE design as an SDP over assignment covariance matrices and then uses Gaussian rounding to obtain binary assignments.
source_refs.author: Thiyageswaran, Kokot, Brennan, Meila, Yu, and Fazel

source_refs.claim: Chen, Li, Deng, and Wang state that an optimized covariance matrix may not correspond to any legitimate joint treatment distribution.
source_refs.author: Chen, Li, Deng, and Wang

source_refs.claim: Bandeira and Kunisky treat the elliptope as the classical relaxation of cut matrices for quadratic optimization over the hypercube.
source_refs.author: Bandeira and Kunisky

source_refs.excerpt: sharp joint-completion LP bounds for network-exposure ADEs with strict cellwise-relaxation nonsharpness

open_problems.why_unsolved: The web literature separates two pieces that the target problem needs together: causal-design papers optimize partitions or relaxed covariance matrices, while elliptope/cut-polytope work describes hypercube relaxation gaps abstractly. Prior CausalSmith network-exposure entries give the analogous warning that marginal or cellwise feasibility can be strictly nonsharp when one shared latent object must realize all constraints, but they do not treat SDP assignment covariances or +-1 second moments.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A finite-sample or asymptotic phase-diagram theorem for a named balanced two-block homophily graph family, giving necessary and sufficient parameter inequalities under which the SDP minimizer is exactly representable as E[ZZ'] for a randomized +-1 assignment and proving positive objective loss outside the region.

writing_exemplar.why: It supplies the motivation arc from interference and homophily to a covariance-SDP design objective and then pivots to implementable rounding.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: network interference makes assignment covariance matter -> homophily and robustness create competing covariance objectives -> the SDP covariance may not be exactly implementable by a +-1 design

method_exemplars.technique: Use optimized covariance design for A/B tests under interference and its treatment-distribution feasibility obstruction as the causal-design reduction target.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The target exactness theorem asks when the optimized covariance is generated by a legitimate randomized treatment assignment, the obstacle highlighted by this paper.

method_exemplars.technique: Use elliptope-versus-cut-matrix Gram geometry to certify whether an SDP solution lies in the convex hull of sign second moments.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The desired iff statement is a cut/elliptope representability certificate specialized to the design objective.

method_exemplars.technique: Adapt graph-elliptope boundary and spectrahedral-face analysis to locate when the objective exposes non-cut boundary points.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The exactness boundary is objective-level exposure of elliptope faces, not generic membership alone.

open_problems.open_problem: Derive the family-specific eta/gamma critical threshold at which a cut-aligned implementable design stops attaining the SDP optimum and a positive +-1 implementability gap begins.

source_refs.claim: The anchor reports design behavior changing across homophily and robustness trade-off ratios, but gives approximation control rather than a sharp exact-attainment threshold.
source_refs.author: Thiyageswaran, Kokot, Brennan, Meila, Yu, and Fazel

source_refs.claim: Leung derives cluster-randomized design rates by balancing interference bias and variance, without resolving elliptope implementability.
source_refs.author: Leung

source_refs.claim: Viviano et al. choose clusters to minimize finite-sample worst-case causal MSE rather than community-detection criteria.
source_refs.author: Viviano, Lei, Imbens, Karrer, Schrijvers, and Shi

source_refs.excerpt: Closed-form scalar threshold T(V1,V4,pbar) separating the symmetric optimum from a skewed global optimum
characterize when the stratified symmetric law delta_pbar is globally optimal by the closed threshold

open_problems.why_unsolved: The causal-design papers identify bias-variance or homophily-robustness tradeoffs, while the prior saturation-design run shows what a scalar exactness threshold should look like in a much simpler graph-free moment program. The unresolved obstacle here is that the threshold must combine the Laplacian term, pseudoinverse-Laplacian term, robustness norm, graph eigenstructure, and cut-polytope feasibility without collapsing to the SDP optimum value itself.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A theorem giving a scalar critical value c_G(kappa) for a regular graph or two-community SBM family such that +-1 exactness holds on one side of eta/gamma = c_G(kappa), fails on the other side, and the formula is expressed in graph invariants such as degree, community eigenvalues, homophily contrast, or Laplacian spectrum.

writing_exemplar.why: Its setup already narrates the transition among clustered, spread, and robust assignment covariances as tuning ratios vary.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: HT-GATE worst-case MSE depends on graph covariance -> eta/gamma tunes cut alignment against homophily spreading -> a critical ratio should mark exactness breakdown

method_exemplars.technique: Use SDP dual certificates and exposed-face arguments from semidefinite relaxations of cut objectives.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The threshold must prove which side of a parameter boundary exposes a cut face versus a non-cut elliptope face.

method_exemplars.technique: Carry over finite-sample worst-case-MSE design decompositions for graph cluster experiments.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The threshold is meaningful because it is a causal MSE design transition, not only a graph-optimization transition.

method_exemplars.technique: Adapt graph-scale bias-variance balancing to express design regimes as graph-family scalar thresholds.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The target eta/gamma threshold has the same design-regime role as the cluster-scale threshold in spatial interference.

open_problems.open_problem: Produce a tight or one-sided-sharp rounding-loss certificate for the price of replacing the elliptope SDP design by an implementable randomized +-1 design.

source_refs.claim: The anchor adapts MAXCUT-style rounding but the design problem is a minimization with a different inequality direction and empirical rounding gaps.
source_refs.author: Thiyageswaran, Kokot, Brennan, Meila, Yu, and Fazel

source_refs.claim: Goemans and Williamson provide the canonical SDP relaxation and randomized hyperplane rounding template for MAXCUT.
source_refs.author: Goemans and Williamson

source_refs.claim: Harshaw, Savje, Spielman, and Zhang provide implementable randomized designs with finite-sample worst-case MSE control.
source_refs.author: Harshaw, Savje, Spielman, and Zhang

source_refs.excerpt: Exact finite-M attainability theorem ... plus an explicit O(1/M + 1/m) rounding-loss bound.
Replace pi_rd with an explicit floor/ceiling split construction

open_problems.why_unsolved: Classical Goemans-Williamson gives a maximization approximation certificate, while the anchor's objective is a mixed minimization involving L, L_dagger, and a robustness norm. Prior CausalSmith saturation work records the same implementation layer in a scalar setting, including reviewer criticism of naive rounding, but it does not provide a cut/elliptope certificate for assignment-covariance SDP designs.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A theorem giving rho*(G, eta/gamma, kappa) such that the best feasible randomized +-1 design is within additive or multiplicative rho* of the elliptope optimum, with equality or one-sided sharpness on a named graph family and zero-loss cells matching the +-1 exactness region.

writing_exemplar.why: It already frames the SDP-to-rounding step and the one-sided nature of the available approximation argument.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: SDP design gives an ideal covariance -> Gaussian rounding makes assignments feasible -> the causal MSE price of implementability needs a sharp certificate

method_exemplars.technique: Adapt semidefinite-relaxation approximation inequalities for nonconvex quadratic optimization to a minimization loss certificate.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The desired rho* is an integrality-gap statement for a quadratic/covariance relaxation.

method_exemplars.technique: Use global-constraint SDP rounding logic to handle assignment-balance or covariance constraints that alter ordinary cut rounding.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The design implementability gap is a constrained cut-type rounding problem, not unconstrained MAXCUT.

method_exemplars.technique: Compare against Gram-Schmidt Walk worst-case MSE bounds for implementable randomized experiments.
method_exemplars.why_closest: A rounding-loss certificate should be benchmarked against an implementable design with causal MSE guarantees.

open_problems.open_problem: Identify the robustness-dominated kappa regime in which balanced Bernoulli or spread-out randomization exactly attains the relaxation optimum, and the neighboring regime where robustness creates positive implementability loss.

source_refs.claim: The anchor explicitly studies interference, homophily, and robustness trade-offs in the covariance-design objective.
source_refs.author: Thiyageswaran, Kokot, Brennan, Meila, Yu, and Fazel

source_refs.claim: Harshaw, Savje, Spielman, and Zhang formalize the randomized-experiment compromise between covariate balance and robustness.
source_refs.author: Harshaw, Savje, Spielman, and Zhang

source_refs.excerpt: Minimax robust first-stage design when V3 is only known to lie in [-Gamma,Gamma]
dropped after scan because it depends too heavily on the unresolved base threshold
V1=0 ties ... V1<0 gives cluster Bernoulli ... V1>0 gives within-cluster stratified

open_problems.why_unsolved: The prior saturation run explicitly dropped its robust first-stage variant because the base exactness threshold was unresolved. The anchor's kappa||X||_q term creates the analogous robustness force in the SDP covariance problem, but the literature currently gives implementable robust designs and relaxed SDP objectives without a theorem saying when the robustness-dominated relaxation is exactly generated by a feasible +-1 assignment law.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A large-kappa exactness theorem for a named regular or two-community graph family proving that balanced Bernoulli or another explicit spread-out +-1 law attains the elliptope optimum, plus a neighboring parameter region where the elliptope chooses a nonimplementable spread matrix and the rounding loss is strictly positive.

writing_exemplar.why: Its motivation explicitly makes robustness a first-class design tradeoff alongside homophily and interference.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: interference rewards graph-aware covariance -> homophily pushes toward cut-aligned designs -> robustness penalizes concentration and may favor spread-out randomization

method_exemplars.technique: Use worst-case MSE and robustness/balance decomposition for implementable randomized experiment designs.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The kappa regime is about causal MSE robustness under a chosen randomization, the same design-based object.

method_exemplars.technique: Use the anchor's trace-plus-norm SDP objective and compare its optimizer against explicit Bernoulli or spread sign-law second moments.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The target theorem resolves a regime of the exact anchor objective.

method_exemplars.technique: Use relaxation-gap inequalities to show when a spread elliptope point cannot be matched by a sign-law covariance.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The failure side is an integrality-gap statement for a robustified SDP relaxation.

open_problems.open_problem: Extend the +-1 exactness and rounding-loss boundary from unconstrained Rademacher assignment laws to fixed-treatment-fraction or globally balanced +-1 designs.

source_refs.claim: Raghavendra and Tan show that adding global cardinality constraints to cut-type CSPs requires specialized SDP hierarchy and rounding analysis.
source_refs.author: Raghavendra and Tan

source_refs.claim: The anchor's SDP rounding discussion is centered on uniform treatment probabilities and leaves broader constrained-design extensions to future work.
source_refs.author: Thiyageswaran, Kokot, Brennan, Meila, Yu, and Fazel

source_refs.claim: Chatterjee, Dey, and Goswami derive precise limiting variance for Gram-Schmidt random walk designs, sharpening earlier upper-bound-only analysis.
source_refs.author: Chatterjee, Dey, and Goswami

source_refs.claim: Harshaw, Savje, Spielman, and Zhang let experimenters navigate robustness and balance through an implementable randomized design.
source_refs.author: Harshaw, Savje, Spielman, and Zhang

open_problems.why_unsolved: Fixed treatment counts change the feasible second-moment polytope by adding cardinality-induced constraints, so an exactness threshold for unconstrained sign vectors can be false under balanced assignment. The causal-design literature often imposes balance operationally, while the SDP/CSP literature warns that global cardinality constraints change rounding behavior; no cited paper gives the balanced +-1 second-moment boundary for the interference-homophily objective.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A balanced-design analogue of the eta/gamma threshold and rho* rounding-loss certificate, with exact feasibility conditions for second moments generated by fixed-cardinality +-1 assignments on regular and two-community SBM graphs.

writing_exemplar.why: It frames why cardinality-constrained cut problems require their own SDP and rounding analysis rather than inheriting unconstrained results.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: ordinary cut relaxations ignore global counts -> experiments often require fixed treatment fractions -> exactness and rounding must be rederived for balanced sign laws

method_exemplars.technique: Use SDP rounding with global cardinality constraints to characterize feasible balanced sign covariances.
method_exemplars.why_closest: Fixed treatment counts turn the implementability question into a cardinality-constrained cut-polytope problem.

method_exemplars.technique: Use implementable balancing designs and their worst-case MSE control as the causal-design benchmark.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The extension is motivated by experimental balance constraints, not abstract cardinality alone.

method_exemplars.technique: Use CLT and limiting-variance analysis for Gram-Schmidt random walk designs to compare balanced implementable designs with SDP relaxations.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The paper supplies limit-law machinery for a concrete balanced randomized design.

literature_map:
Core design-based interference anchors: HudgensHalloran2008 (JASA, partial interference), AronowSamii2017 (Annals of Applied Statistics, exposure mappings and HT inference), UganderKarrerBackstromKleinberg2013 (KDD, graph cluster randomization), and BasseAiroldi2018Limitations (Annals of Applied Statistics/arXiv, limitations under arbitrary/network interference).
Optimal/robust design anchors: BairdBohrenMcIntoshOzler2018 (Review of Economics and Statistics, saturation design under interference), BasseAiroldi2018Model (Biometrika, model-assisted network-correlated design), Leung2022 (rate-optimal cluster-randomized spatial-interference designs), and VivianoLeiImbensKarrerSchrijversShi2026 (causal clustering by finite-sample worst-case MSE).
Covariance-design anchors: ChenLiDengWang2024 (NeurIPS, optimized covariance design for A/B testing under interference) names the implementability problem for optimized covariances; ThiyageswaranKokotBrennanMeilaYuFazel2026 (arXiv:2601.17145) is the direct SDP/Gaussian-rounding HT-GATE worst-case-MSE anchor under interference, homophily, and robustness.
Cluster/homophily tension: CortezRodriguezEichhornYu2024 studies two-stage rollout clustering and emphasizes the tension between minimizing cut edges and achieving balance under homophily; KarrerEtAl2021 provides deployed network-experimentation context.
Implementable robust designs: HarshawSavjeSpielmanZhang2024 gives Gram-Schmidt Walk balance/robustness guarantees; ChatterjeeDeyGoswami2023 supplies limiting-variance theory for that implementable design class.
SDP/elliptope/cut-polytope machinery: GoemansWilliamson1995 supplies the hyperplane-rounding template; Nesterov1998 gives semidefinite-relaxation approximation tools; RaghavendraTan2012 shows global cardinality constraints require distinct SDP rounding analysis.
Elliptope boundary geometry: BandeiraKunisky2018 studies generalized elliptope/cut-matrix geometry; LaurentMascarinTelen2026 develops graph-elliptope algebraic-boundary tools relevant to objective-exposed non-cut faces.
The live tension is that causal design objectives are finite-population, design-based MSE criteria, while the relaxation gap is cut/elliptope geometry; no cited paper gives graph-family exactness regions, eta/gamma thresholds, or sharp rounding-loss certificates for the anchor objective.
prior_proposal_map:
Inspected under doc/formalization only, no web. Active qid exp_design_pm1_exactness_boundary_v1 had only state.json and pipeline.jsonl init/state metadata, no substantive prior-proposal content.
Highest-yield prior was _bank/downgraded/exp_saturation_skew_threshold_v1 plus _restart_archive/exp_saturation_skew_threshold_FAILED_proposal_1782548511: yielded scalar threshold, finite-M attainability, rounding-loss, SUTVA Bernoulli/stratified corner, robust-design dropped angle, §11 honest scope, reviewer incremental/already-known calls, and bank degeneracy caveat.
_bank/candidates/pid_network_exposure_v1 yielded shared-completion versus cellwise-relaxation nonsharpness and reviewer strengthening paths about signed feasibility certificates.
_bank/failed/pid_network_random_set_v1 yielded failed sparse-network witness lessons: relaxed/cellwise feasibility can be ill-posed or empty, certificates must encode a shared feasible object.
_bank/failed/pid_network_exposure_aliasing_graphpair_v1 yielded negative guidance: finite graph-pair witnesses are useful sanity checks but frontier claims fail if they reduce to definitional quotient membership.
_bank/downgraded/exp_ego_cluster_exposure_clt_ego_cluster_polynomial_clt yielded exact HT exposure probability/covariance infrastructure but honest scope says it does not solve design over all clusterings; variance-envelope sharpness is completion-sharp, not estimator/design optimality.
No direct prior SDP/elliptope/cut-polytope proposal was found; rg matches for elliptope, cut-polytope, Goemans-Williamson, SDP, design covariance, and Rademacher/sign-vector terms were current qid metadata, generic Rademacher empirical-process notes, or unrelated sign-flip panel material.
